UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores additional information about a user, including roles and metadata, complementing Firebase Authentication data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. Corresponds to the Firebase Authentication User ID.

role.description: The assigned role for the user (e.g., Viewer, Verifier, Manager, Admin, SuperAdmin).

photoUrl.description: URL to the user's profile photo.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Asset.title: Asset
Asset.description: Represents a single physical asset managed by the system, including its details, status, and traceability.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Asset entity.

serialNumber.description: The unique serial number of the asset.

model.description: The model name or identifier of the asset.

location.description: The current physical location of the asset.

description.description: A general description of the asset.

conditionNotes.description: Detailed notes about the asset's current physical condition.

status.description: The current operational status of the asset (e.g., Active, Disposed, Under Repair, Missing).

assetType.description: The category or type of asset, often derived from the source Excel sheet name (e.g., Ntb far, Motorcycles, GeneXpert).

grantId.description: Reference to the Grant entity under which this asset is managed. (Relationship: Grant 1:N Asset)

sourceWorkbookId.description: Reference to the WorkbookSource entity from which this asset was originally imported. (Relationship: WorkbookSource 1:N Asset)

sourceSheetId.description: Reference to the SheetSource entity from which this asset was imported within the workbook. (Relationship: SheetSource 1:N Asset)

sourceRowNumber.description: The original row number in the source Excel sheet for traceability.

sourceSectionContext.description: Contextual information from merged cells or section headers in the source Excel sheet for traceability.

attachmentIds.description: References to Attachment entities associated with this asset. (Relationship: Asset N:N Attachment)

latestVerificationId.description: Reference to the latest AssetVerification entity for this asset. (Relationship: Asset 1:1 AssetVerification - latest)

createdByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that created this asset record. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Asset)

updatedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that last updated this asset record. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Asset)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the asset record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the asset record was last updated.

ocrData.description: A JSON string containing raw data extracted by OCR from asset labels, for review and audit.

AssetVerification.title: AssetVerification
AssetVerification.description: Records a specific physical verification event for an asset, including status, findings, and verifier details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AssetVerification entity.

assetId.description: Reference to the Asset entity that was verified. (Relationship: Asset 1:N AssetVerification)

verifiedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that performed the verification. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N AssetVerification)

verificationDate.description: The date and time when the physical verification was performed.

status.description: The outcome status of the verification (e.g., Verified, Pending, Discrepancy, Unavailable).

notes.description: Detailed notes recorded during the verification, including discrepancies or observations.

foundLocation.description: The actual location where the asset was found during verification.

attachmentIds.description: References to Attachment entities (e.g., photos of the asset) captured during verification. (Relationship: AssetVerification N:N Attachment)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the verification record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the verification record was last updated.

Attachment.title: Attachment
Attachment.description: Stores metadata for files (photos, documents) uploaded to Firebase Storage and linked to other entities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Attachment entity.

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

fileType.description: The MIME type of the file (e.g., image/jpeg, application/pdf).

storagePath.description: The full path to the file within Firebase Storage.

downloadUrl.description: A public URL to download the file from Firebase Storage.

size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that uploaded the file. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Attachment)

associatedAssetId.description: Reference to the Asset entity this attachment is directly linked to. (Relationship: Asset 1:N Attachment)

associatedVerificationId.description: Reference to the AssetVerification entity this attachment is linked to. (Relationship: AssetVerification 1:N Attachment)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the attachment record was created.

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Records all significant system actions, data changes, and user activities for traceability and compliance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AuditLog entry.

action.description: Describes the type of action performed (e.g., AssetCreated, AssetUpdated, UserRoleChanged, ImportInitiated, Login).

entityType.description: The type of entity affected by the action (e.g., Asset, UserProfile, Grant).

entityId.description: The ID of the specific entity affected by the action. Null if the action doesn't relate to a specific entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that initiated the action. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N AuditLog)

timestamp.description: The date and time when the action occurred.

beforeState.description: A JSON string representing the state of the entity before the action (for updates/deletes).

afterState.description: A JSON string representing the state of the entity after the action (for creations/updates).

details.description: Additional relevant details about the action or system event.

ipAddress.description: The IP address from which the action originated.

Grant.title: Grant
Grant.description: Defines a grant or project under which a collection of assets is managed and tracked.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Grant entity.

name.description: The official name of the grant or project.

description.description: A detailed description of the grant or project scope.

startDate.description: The official start date of the grant period.

endDate.description: The official end date of the grant period.

status.description: The current status of the grant (e.g., Active, Completed, Archived, Upcoming).

createdByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that created this grant record. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Grant)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the grant record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the grant record was last updated.

WorkbookSource.title: WorkbookSource
WorkbookSource.description: Tracks metadata about an Excel workbook file uploaded for asset import, maintaining source traceability.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkbookSource entity.

fileName.description: The original filename of the uploaded Excel workbook.

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the workbook was uploaded.

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that uploaded the workbook. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N WorkbookSource)

storagePath.description: The path to the uploaded workbook file in Firebase Storage.

status.description: The processing status of the workbook (e.g., Uploaded, Processing, Processed, Failed).

processingLogs.description: A summary of parsing and import results, including any warnings or errors.

originalWorkbookHash.description: A hash of the original workbook content to detect duplicate uploads.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the workbook source record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the workbook source record was last updated.

SheetSource.title: SheetSource
SheetSource.description: Tracks metadata about a specific sheet within an imported Excel workbook, crucial for sheet-specific parsing logic and traceability.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SheetSource entity.

workbookId.description: Reference to the WorkbookSource entity this sheet belongs to. (Relationship: WorkbookSource 1:N SheetSource)

sheetName.description: The name of the sheet within the Excel workbook (e.g., Ntb far, Motorcycles).

parsingLogicIdentifier.description: An identifier for the specific parsing logic or schema applied to this sheet.

totalRecordsProcessed.description: The total number of asset records processed from this sheet.

totalRecordsImported.description: The number of asset records successfully imported from this sheet.

failedRecordsCount.description: The number of records that failed to import from this sheet.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the sheet source record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the sheet source record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores additional information about a user, including their assigned role. Access is restricted to the user themselves (for their own profile) or to 'SuperAdmin' for full management.
An empty document exists here if the user has a 'SuperAdmin' role. Used for fast, atomic authorization checks in security rules, adhering to DBAC and Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: An empty document exists here if the user has an 'Admin' role. Used for fast, atomic authorization checks in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: An empty document exists here if the user has a 'Manager' role. Used for fast, atomic authorization checks in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: An empty document exists here if the user has a 'Verifier' role. Used for fast, atomic authorization checks in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user.

definition.description: Top-level collection for managing grants or projects. Access is typically for 'Manager' roles and above, based on `createdByUserId` and global role checks.

params.name: grantId
params.description: The unique ID of the grant.

definition.description: Stores metadata about uploaded Excel workbooks. Primarily accessible and manageable by 'Admin' roles and above. Includes `uploadedByUserId` for ownership traceability.

params.name: workbookId
params.description: The unique ID of the workbook.

definition.description: Stores metadata about individual sheets within an uploaded workbook. Includes denormalized `uploadedByUserId` from its parent workbook for Authorization Independence, enabling rule checks without `get()` calls.

params.name: workbookId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent workbook.

params.name: sheetId
params.description: The unique ID of the sheet within the workbook.

definition.description: Core asset records, logically grouped under their respective grants. Access is determined by application-wide roles (e.g., 'Manager' for full management, 'Verifier' for verification-related actions). Includes `createdByUserId` and `grantId` (from path) for authorization.

params.name: grantId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent grant.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique ID of the asset.

definition.description: Records physical verification events for assets, nested hierarchically. Includes `verifiedByUserId` for ownership and `grantId` and `assetId` (from path) for context. Denormalizes `assetCreatedByUserId` (owner of the parent asset) for Authorization Independence.

params.name: grantId
params.description: The unique ID of the grant associated with the asset.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent asset.

params.name: verificationId
params.description: The unique ID of the verification record.

definition.description: Stores metadata for files (photos, documents) uploaded to Firebase Storage. Includes `uploadedByUserId`. For Authorization Independence, it denormalizes `grantContextId`, `assetContextId`, and `verificationContextId` from its associated primary entity to allow direct access checks without parent lookups. A single attachment should typically have a primary association for security.

params.name: attachmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the attachment.

definition.description: Immutable records of all significant system actions and data changes. Write access is strictly limited to server-side processes (e.g., Firebase Functions) via Admin SDK. Read access is restricted to 'Admin' and 'SuperAdmin' roles.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique ID of the audit log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for Assetain is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence and Query-Aware Security Rules (QAPs), ensuring scalability, security, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   **Role-Based Access Control (DBAC):** Instead of storing roles only within `UserProfile` documents and using `get()` calls in security rules (which would break atomic operations and be hard to debug), dedicated collections like `/app_roles_super_admins/{userId}` are used. An empty document exists in these collections if a user possesses the corresponding role. This enables highly efficient `exists()` checks in security rules, fulfilling Authorization Independence.
*   **Nested Data Context:** For entities that are conceptually nested but require independent authorization, critical parent data is denormalized. For example, `SheetSource` documents include `uploadedByUserId` (denormalized from its parent `WorkbookSource`) and `AssetVerification` documents include `assetCreatedByUserId` (denormalized from the parent `Asset`). This allows rules to evaluate access to a `SheetSource` or `AssetVerification` document without needing to perform a `get()` call to its parent document.
*   **Attachment Authorization:** Attachments can be linked to either `Asset` or `AssetVerification` entities. To support flexible and secure access without `get()`, each `Attachment` document denormalizes the `grantContextId`, `assetContextId`, and `verificationContextId` from its associated primary entity. This enables security rules to determine access based on the attachment's context (e.g., who can access the associated asset/grant) directly from the attachment document itself, without requiring additional lookups.

**Query-Aware Security Rules (QAPs):**
*   **Structural Segregation:** Data is segregated into distinct collections based on its security posture. For instance, `WorkbookSource` and `SheetSource` are administrative collections, `grants` are separate, and assets are nested under grants. This makes it straightforward to write rules that allow `list` operations, as all documents within a given collection or subcollection share a consistent access model.
*   **Hierarchical Paths:** Assets are structured hierarchically under grants (`/grants/{grantId}/assets/{assetId}`), and verifications are nested under assets (`/grants/{grantId}/assets/{assetId}/verifications/{verificationId}`). This inherently provides `grantId` and `assetId` as path parameters to security rules, allowing for efficient filtering by these IDs in queries and enforcing hierarchical access without complex rule logic. For example, a user can `list` all assets within a specific grant if they are authorized for that grant context.
*   **Denormalized Fields for Filtering:** Key fields such as `createdByUserId`, `uploadedByUserId`, `grantId`, `assetId`, and various `contextId` fields in `Attachment` documents are directly queryable. This allows client-side `list` queries to include `where` clauses (e.g., `where('grantId', '==', 'abc')`) which can then be securely validated by Firestore rules, ensuring that the rules are not acting as filters but rather as enforcers of already-filtered queries.
